L2.description: The package uses a non-cryptographic pseudorandom number generator to select characters when creating one-time passwords, making the generated passwords predictable and vulnerable to brute-force attacks.

L3.area: OTP character assembly and random index generation within the core generator module
L3.description: The one-time password generator relies on JavaScript's standard non-cryptographic pseudorandom number generator to produce random indices for character selection during OTP creation, making generated passwords statistically predictable and susceptible to brute-force attacks.

ground_truth.reason: The rand function uses Math.random() instead of a cryptographically secure random number generator to select characters for one-time passwords, making the generated OTPs predictable and vulnerable to brute-force attacks.
